reason: The item cleanly targets boundary-aware reasoning about SOC-dependent explosion metrics: 50% SOC can improve certain indicators (e.g., LEL) yet cannot be framed as eliminating risk, and it preserves the stated non-monotonicity complication. Decoys are mostly single-fault and engineering-plausible (mechanism inversion, cross-chemistry overgeneralization, and overclaiming sufficiency from one metric), creating meaningful discrimination for experts. Minor weaknesses are that some options are denser/compound (notably B/F) and a few phrasing cues (“fully justified,” “for all chemistries”) make elimination slightly easier than ideal.
